$description: Button component color tokens for all button variants and states.

danger-active.$description: Background color for danger buttons in active/pressed state. Use when user clicks or presses a destructive action button.

danger-hover.$description: Background color for danger buttons on hover. Use when user hovers over a destructive action button to provide visual feedback.

danger-primary.$description: Background color for primary danger buttons in default state. Use for destructive actions like delete, remove, or cancel that require high emphasis.

danger-secondary.$description: Border and text color for secondary danger buttons. Use for destructive actions that need less emphasis than primary danger buttons.

disabled.$description: Background color for disabled buttons across all variants. Use when a button action is temporarily unavailable or not applicable in the current context.

primary.$description: Background color for primary buttons in default state. Use for the main call-to-action on a page or in a workflow step.

primary-active.$description: Background color for primary buttons in active/pressed state. Use when user clicks or presses the primary action button.

primary-hover.$description: Background color for primary buttons on hover. Use when user hovers over the primary action button to provide visual feedback.

secondary.$description: Background color for secondary buttons in default state. Use for secondary actions that support the primary action or provide alternative paths.

secondary-active.$description: Background color for secondary buttons in active/pressed state. Use when user clicks or presses a secondary action button.

secondary-hover.$description: Background color for secondary buttons on hover. Use when user hovers over a secondary action button to provide visual feedback.

separator.$description: Border color for button separators in button groups or split buttons. Use to visually divide adjacent buttons while maintaining their connection.

tertiary.$description: Text and icon color for tertiary buttons in default state. Use for low-emphasis actions or when multiple actions need equal visual weight.

tertiary-active.$description: Text and icon color for tertiary buttons in active/pressed state. Use when user clicks or presses a tertiary action button.

tertiary-hover.$description: Background color for tertiary buttons on hover. Use when user hovers over a tertiary action button to provide subtle visual feedback.
